profile.name: Pardhu Sri Rushi Varma Konduru
profile.bio: Exploring statistical machine learning, autonomous systems. Building modular, interpretable, and compositionally safe intelligence systems with a focus on behavioral robustness, reversibility, adaptation, and stability in autonomous driving and adaptive world models.

about.title: Research Philosophy
paragraphs: I am an independent researcher and final-year cybersecurity undergrad working at the intersection of machine learning, AI security, and adaptive systems.

I am developing a strong foundation in statistical machine learning and applied mathematics, particularly probability theory, optimization, and information theory and applying these concepts to my research on latent behavioral structures in low-dimensional statistical manifolds for autonomous driving systems. My work explores how learning dynamics, manifold geometry, and distributional shifts shape behavioral adaptation, stability, interpretability, and robustness in autonomous world models and decision-making driving agents.

My research interests include statistical and robust machine learning, adversarial machine learning, structural robustness of adaptive models, AI safety and controllability, and reliable learning systems.
My goal is to contribute to the design of trustworthy machine learning systems that remain stable, interpretable, and controllable under continuous adaptation.

researchAreas.title: Latent Behavioral Statistical Manifolds
researchAreas.subtitle: Statistical Learning & Behavioral Manifolds
researchAreas.description: Investigating the manifold geometry of adaptive agent telemetry through statistical learning, latent state-space modeling, regime separability, and behavioral dynamics in evolving autonomous systems.
researchAreas.keywords: latent manifolds • statistical learning • regime separability • behavioral telemetry

publications.title: Latent Behavioral Structures in Low Dimensional Statistical Manifolds
publications.abstract: LBSM is an ongoing statistical machine learning research investigating whether adaptive behavioral telemetry generated by autonomous systems exhibits structured low-dimensional latent organization despite high-dimensional observable dynamics.

systems.name: LBSM
systems.description: Latent Behavioral State Machines: A statistical learning and behavioral manifold research framework for modeling adaptive agent telemetry. Explores latent state-space geometry, regime separability, behavioral transitions, and manifold dynamics in evolving autonomous systems through dimensionality reduction, clustering, and telemetry-driven analysis.

systems.name: Project Ouroboros
systems.description: Modular ESP32-based cybersecurity platform for wireless defense, monitoring, and controlled research. Hardware and firmware modules for cyber-physical security experimentation, featuring embedded systems integration and real-time RF threat response capabilities.

notes.title: Thought..
notes.description: Perhaps we are only vessels of a greater curiosity..

contact.collaborationText: Open to research collaboration and academic discussion. Focused on contributing to machine learning research and advancing the field through robust, trustworthy, and adaptive systems.
contact.notes: Perhaps we are only vessels of a greater curiosity
